req.title: Reset employee password — variant 4
req.statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-105 defines four testable behaviours: (1) successful reset with a valid token, (2) rejection of an expired token (410), (3) rejection of a password that violates policy (422), and (4) rate-limiting per user. I model these with EP for the happy-path, NEG for expired token and policy violation, BVA for the token at the exact 30-minute boundary, STC for session-invalidation state transition, and DT for rate-limit enforcement. Each test uses fixtures and helper placeholders so the executable stays within 40 lines and remains idempotent.

tests.name: test_valid_token_resets_password_and_invalidates_sessions
preconditions: Employee account exists with at least one active session
A fresh reset token (age < 30 min) has been issued for that employee
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token and policy-compliant new password
expected: Password is updated; subsequent login with new password succeeds
All prior sessions are invalidated (old session cookie returns 401)

tests.name: test_expired_token_returns_410
preconditions: A reset token aged more than 30 minutes has been issued (simulated via test helper)
actions: POST /password/reset with expired token and policy-compliant new password
expected: Password is NOT changed; login with old password still succeeds
trace: R-WEB-105: expired links must be rejected

tests.name: test_token_at_exactly_30_minutes_is_rejected
preconditions: A reset token aged exactly 30 minutes (1800 seconds) has been issued
actions: POST /password/reset with token whose age == 30 min exactly
expected: HTTP 410 Gone returned (token at boundary is considered expired)
trace: R-WEB-105: link expires AFTER 30 minutes — at exactly 30 min the window is closed

tests.name: test_password_violating_policy_returns_422
preconditions: A fresh reset token has been issued
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token but weak password (e.g. 'abc')
expected: Response body contains password policy error details

tests.name: test_rate_limit_blocks_excessive_reset_requests
preconditions: Rate-limit counter for the employee is clean (no recent requests)
actions: POST /password/reset N+1 times where N is the documented per-user rate limit
Verify that the first N requests are processed (200 or 4xx for invalid tokens) and the (N+1)th is rate-limited
expected: Requests within the rate limit return non-429 status codes
The request exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Retry-After header is present on 429 response
trace: R-WEB-105: operation must be rate-limited per user
